title: 4d printing smart culture substrate for cell growth
abstract: Disclosed herein is a 4D printed programmable culture substrate with the self-morphing ability to accommodate the change in morphology of stem cells during differentiation. The 4D printed culture substrate includes a shape memory polymer that is configured for transformation from a first topographical shape to a second topographical shape during a predetermined time period in response to a stimulus, such as temperature. The first topographical shape may include micro- wells and the second topographical shape may include microgrooves, which can accommodate the growth and differentiation of neural stem cells.
claims: Claims Claims What is claimed is: 1. A substrate for cell growth comprising a shape memory polymer having a first topographical shape, said shape memory polymer configured for transformation from the first topographical shape to a predetermined second topographical shape during a predetermined time period in response to a stimulus, each of the first topographical shape and the second topographical shape comprising cell receiving portions. 2. The substrate of claim 1, wherein the shape memory polymer comprises a combination of a stiff monomer, a flexible crosslinker and a crosslinking modulator. 3. The substrate of claim 2, wherein the stiff monomer is a bisphenol epoxides, the flexible crosslinker is a polyether with terminal amine functional groups and the crosslinking modulator is an alkyl amine. 4. The substrate of claim 2, wherein the stiff monomer is bisphenol A diglycidyl ether, the flexible crosslinker is poly(propylene glycol) bis(2-aminopropyl) ether and the crosslinking modulator is decylamine. 5. The substrate of claim 1, wherein the first topographical shape comprises micro-wells and the second topographical shape comprises microgrooves. 6. The substrate of claim 5, wherein the micro-wells have a diameter ranging from 200 pm to 1 mm. 7. The substrate of claim 5, wherein the micro-grooves have a width ranging from 10 pm to 200 pm. 8. The substrate of claim 1, wherein the substrate is printed via 4D printing, and wherein the 4D printed substrate was initially printed with the second topographical shape, but was transformed to the first topographical shape by imprinting. 9. The substrate of claim 8, wherein the second topographical shape is stable at a temperature range from 23 Â°C to 37 Â°C prior to the imprinting. 10. The substrate of claim 8, wherein the first topographical shape was fixed at a temperature of about 23 Â°C after the imprinting. 11. The substrate of claim 1, wherein the predetermined time period is between 1 and 30 days. 12. The substrate of claim 1, wherein the predetermined time period is 15 days. 13. The substrate of claim 1, wherein the stimulus is a change in temperature. 14. The substrate of claim 13, wherein the change in temperature comprises a change from 23 Â°C to 37Â°C. 15. A system for cell growth comprising a 4D printed culture substrate comprising a shape memory polymer that is configured for transformation from a first topographical shape to a second topographical shape during a predetermined time period in response to a stimulus. 16. The system of claim 15, wherein the shape memory polymer comprises a combination of a stiff monomer, a flexible crosslinker and a crosslinking modulator. 17. The system of claim 16, wherein the stiff monomer is a bisphenol epoxides, the flexible crosslinker is a polyether with terminal amine functional groups and the crosslinking modulator is an alkyl amine. 18. The system of claim 16, wherein the stiff monomer is bisphenol A diglycidyl ether, the flexible crosslinker is poly(propylene glycol) bis(2-aminopropyl) ether and the crosslinking modulator is decylamine. 19. The system of claim 15, wherein the first topographical shape is comprised of micro- wells and the second topographical shape is comprised of microgrooves. 20. The system of claim 19, wherein the micro-wells have a diameter ranging from 200 pm to 1 mm. 21. The system of claim 19, wherein the micro-grooves have a width ranging from 10 pm to 200 pm. 22. The system of claim 15, wherein the 4D printed culture substrate was initially printed with the second topographical shape, but was transformed to the first topographical shape by imprinting at 60 Â°C. 23. The system of claim 15, wherein the second topographical shape is stable at a temperature range from 23 Â°C to 37 Â°C prior to the imprinting. 24. The system of claim 23, wherein the first topographical shape was fixed at a temperature of about 23 Â°C after the imprinting. 25. The system of claim 15, wherein the predetermined time period is between 3 minutes to 6 months. 26. The system of claim 15, wherein the predetermined time period is 15 days. 27. The system of claim 15, wherein the stimulus is a change in temperature. 28. The system of claim 27, wherein the change in temperature is from 23Â°C to 37Â°C. 29. The system of claim 15, further comprising neural stem cells growing on the 4D printed culture substrate. 30. A method of producing a 4D printed culture substrate system comprising: fabricating a 4D printed culture substrate via extrusion to form a second topographical shape of the 4D printed culture substrate; and thermomechanical reprogramming of the 4D printed culture substrate to form the first topographical shape of the 4D printed culture substrate. 31. The method of claim 30, wherein the fabrication of the 4D printed culture substrate comprises the steps of: extruding the shape material polymer into a mold with micro-grooves having a width between 10 pm to 200 pm; and curing the prepared 4D material within the mold. 32. The method of claim 30, wherein the thermomechanical reprogramming of the 4D printed culture substrate comprises the steps of: imprinting the 4D printed culture substrate with a micro-pillar mold with micro pillars having a diameter between 200 pm to 1 mm; and maintaining the 4D printed culture substrate at about 20 Â°C to 25 Â°C to fix the first topographical shape of the 4D printed culture substrate. 33. A method of using a 4D printed culture substrate system comprising: obtaining a 4D printed culture substrate system having a first topographical shape comprising of micro-wells which is maintained at from about 23 Â°C to 25 Â°C; and culturing cells on the micro-wells of the 4D printed culture substrate system under standard cell culture conditions, wherein the 4D printed culture substrate undergoes a transformation of topographical shape during the said period from micro-wells to micro-grooves. 34. The method of claim 33, wherein the cells are adherent stem cells. 35. The method of claim 33, wherein the adherent cells are mammalian in nature. 36. The method of claim 33, wherein the adherent cells are human in nature. 37. The method of claim 33, wherein the adherent cells are obtained from a mouse. 38. The method of claim 33, wherein the adherent cells are stem cells. 39. The method of claim 33, wherein the stem cells are neural stem cells from mammalian neuroectoderm. 40. The method of claim 39, wherein the mammalian neuroectoderm is human neuroectoderm. 41. The method of claim 39, wherein the mammalian neuroectoderm is mouse neuroectoderm. 42. The method of claim 39, wherein the neural stem cells are grown in a neurogenic cell culture medium.
